name: Investigar capacidades completas de Trifecta F1 + oracle y mejorar indexación para paper-writer

description: Full Trifecta F1 capability audit: 7 MCP tools discovered, 3 structural blockers identified, AST layer works on paper-writer

Extended GraphIndexer with configurable source_roots via trifecta_config.json. Paper-writer went from 0/0 to 98 nodes / 55 edges. Added 3 new tests. All 41 graph+oracle tests pass. Graph blocker resolved.

Fixed context_pack.json backward compat: ContextChunk auto-derives id/char_count/token_est from text, ContextPack auto-builds index from chunks and accepts old 'version' key. PRIME+Graph Oracle fully operational on paper-writer. Pre-existing test_build_context_pack failures confirmed unrelated.

Implemented real HybridDispatcher: MCP JSON-RPC over Unix socket to F1 daemon. Creates missing calibration_use_case and discovery_service stubs. Verified ctx_search, ctx_oracle, ctx_health via live daemon. 7 new unit tests for dispatcher. All 50 tests pass.

Extended query classifier with 4 new semantic patterns (definition of, type of, definición de, tipo de) to unlock LSP trigger. Wired LSP client into Oracle CLI path. Added did_open before hover in Oracle. LSP signal fully verified via ast hover (capability_state=FULL). Oracle LSP path gated by old pack source_path quality — pack uses virtual paths like "paper-writer/X" instead of real file paths. 50/50 tests pass.

Scaffolded minimal segment canon for paper-writer (skill.md, AGENTS.md, agent/prime/session _paper-writer.md). Extended BuildContextPackUseCase to read source_roots from config for pack generation. Fixed ContextService.search to use chunk.source_path instead of title_path_norm in SearchHit — now Python source files resolve correctly. Regenerated pack: 134 chunks (was 18). Oracle achieves fidelity=degraded with PRIME+AST+Graph fusion verified. LSP signal circuit complete but gated by pyright cold-start latency (215ms > 20ms budget). Increased LSP budget to 500ms.

Achieved fidelity=full in Oracle! Root cause: pyright returns None when hover character=0 (at keyword) but returns data when character lands on the identifier. Fixed by: (1) adding col_offset to SymbolInfo in AST parser, (2) shifting LSP hover position past "class " (6) or "def " (4) keyword to land on the symbol name. Verified: "what is OrchestratorDependencies" → fidelity=full with PRIME+AST+LSP hover returning class docstring. 50/50 tests pass.

Two-pass graph indexing with global symbol table for cross-file call resolution. Paper-writer: 98/55 nodes/edges → 106/78 (+23 cross-file edges). build_orchestrator_dependencies now has 12 callees across harness/ and integrations/. main()→Orchestrator cross-file edge visible via Oracle. 53 tests pass (3 new cross-file tests). No overfitting: same-file tests unchanged, new tests verify new capability only.

Method-level graph indexing: AST parser extracts class methods (kind=method/init) with qualified_name like Orchestrator.execute. Graph indexer tracks self.method() intra-class calls and method→function cross-kind edges. _DirectCallCollector detects self.attr() patterns. Paper-writer: 106→230 nodes (+124 methods), 78→167 edges (+89 method edges). Orchestrator internal call chain now visible: execute→_run_gate_verification→_run_wrapper_gate→_build_wrapper_artifacts. StateManager.set_gate→load_state,save_state. 3 new unit tests, 55 total pass.

Context-aware graph disambiguation: Oracle uses PRIME hit file paths to prefer graph candidates from the same context directory. When ambiguous target (e.g., 'execute' in both Orchestrator and SkillAdapter) has multiple candidates, and the first yields 0 results, falls through to the next candidate. Also uses qualified_name (e.g., Orchestrator.execute) instead of symbol_name for node_id construction. Verified: 'callees of execute' correctly resolves to Orchestrator.execute (6 callees: _validate_preconditions, _run_gate_verification, _get_next_stage, _build_fail_result, ManuscriptState, OrchestratorResult). 2 new unit tests. 57 total pass.

Transitive graph queries via BFS traversal with configurable depth. Query classifier detects 4 new transitive patterns ('all callers of', 'depends on', 'dependencies of', 'everything X calls') → auto depth=3. GraphStore adds get_callers/callees_transitive with BFS. GraphService.callers/callees accept depth param. CLI adds --depth flag. Oracle wires depth from predicate through disambiguation path. Paper-writer: 'all callees of execute' returns 20 nodes (vs 6 direct), discovering full Orchestrator call chain: execute→_run_gate_verification→_run_wrapper_gate→_build_wrapper_artifacts; validate_repo_initialized→run_gate→Check, GateResult. 7 new unit tests (2 transitive graph, 5 classifier), 84 total pass.

Context-aware fidelity semantics: Oracle now reports fidelity correctly based on which signals were EXPECTED (from query classification), not which signals happened to fire. Relational-only queries ('callers of X') report full when graph+PRIME+AST succeed, because LSP is not applicable. Semantic queries ('what is X') report degraded when LSP fails. Plain search queries report fallback. Query classified once and reused across graph/LSP signal paths. Paper-writer: 'callees of execute' now fidelity=full (was degraded). 'what is OrchestratorDependencies' with LSP → full. 4 new fidelity unit tests, 88 total pass.

Import-level graph edges: file→file dependency tracking alongside function-level calls. _ImportCollector extracts 'import X' and 'from X import Y' statements. Module path resolution maps Python dotted names to project files. Module nodes (kind=module) created per file. GraphStore.get_importers/import_targets_for_node with parametric edge_kind filter. GraphService.importers/import_targets. CLI: 'graph importers'/'graph import-targets' subcommands. Paper-writer: 264 nodes (+34 modules), 209 edges (167 calls + 42 imports). Orchestrator→state, action_runner, artifact_checker, tool_wrapper dependencies now visible. 1 new unit test, 89 total pass.

Oracle now answers import-level dependency queries from natural language. Extended RelationalPredicate with importers/import_targets relation types. Added 6 query classifier patterns for import relations: 'files that import X' (importers), 'who imports X' (importers), 'modules that use X' (importers), 'what depends on X' (importers), 'imports of X' (import_targets), 'what does X import' (import_targets). Oracle graph signal routes importers/import_targets to GraphService.importers/import_targets. Module node disambiguation: import queries prefer kind=module nodes over class/function nodes, preventing PRIME context from overriding. Fixed missing 'if predicate is None' guard on transitive caller patterns that caused import patterns to be overridden. Paper-writer: 'what does orchestrator import' returns 6 module imports (state, action_runner, artifact_checker, tool_wrapper, gates, state_manager). 'files that import orchestrator' returns cli/paper/main.py. 7 new tests, 95 total pass.

Inheritance edges: 3rd graph dimension alongside calls and imports. ClassDef.bases extracted from AST, resolved against global symbol table for cross-file inheritance. Paper-writer: 264 nodes, 219 edges (167 calls + 42 imports + 10 inherits). 7 ToolWrapper subclasses discovered (BibliographyNormalizer, PandocRenderer, RefsMetadataValidator, RefsValidator, ReportingAuditor, StyleLinter, ZoteroImporter). YamlFileStateRepository→StateRepository. GraphStore.get_subclasses/parents_for_node with edge_kind='inherits'. GraphService.subclasses/parents. CLI: graph subclasses/parents. 7 query classifier patterns for inheritance: 'subclasses of X', 'implementations of X', 'what extends X', 'who extends X', 'parents of X', 'what does X extend', 'what does X inherit from'. Oracle routes inheritance through graph signal. 7 new tests, 101 total pass.

Cycle detection: DFS three-color algorithm for calls/imports/inherits. GraphStore.detect_cycles uses white/gray/black DFS to find circular dependencies in any edge kind. GraphStore.find_node_by_id resolves node IDs to node info. GraphService.cycles with edge_kind parameter. CLI: 'trifecta graph cycles --kind calls|imports|inherits'. Paper-writer: 0 cycles across all 3 edge kinds — confirms clean architecture (no circular imports, no recursive calls, no inheritance cycles). Verified with synthetic 3-node cycle test (alpha→beta→gamma→alpha detected correctly) and acyclic test (0 cycles). 2 new unit tests, 103 total pass.

Natural language cycle detection through Oracle. CyclePredicate in query classifier with 8 patterns: 'circular imports' (imports), 'import cycles' (imports), 'call cycles' (calls), 'inheritance cycles' (inherits), 'are there any cycles?' (all), 'dependency cycles' (all), 'circular dependencies' (all), 'check for cycles' (all). Oracle._execute_cycle_signal routes to GraphService.cycles with edge_kind from predicate. Supports 'all' mode that checks all 3 edge kinds. Fidelity=full for successful cycle detection. Paper-writer: 'circular imports' → fidelity=full, 0 cycles. 'are there any cycles?' → fidelity=full, 0 cycles across all 3 kinds. Verified with synthetic 2-node import cycle (mod_a↔mod_b) detected correctly. 9 new tests (8 classifier + 1 integration), 111 total pass.

Orphan detection: find symbols with zero incoming edges (dead code analysis). GraphStore.find_orphans detects nodes with no callers/importers/subclasses across all edge kinds, excluding module nodes (entry points). GraphService.orphans with node detail resolution. CLI: 'trifecta graph orphans'. OrphanPredicate in query classifier with 8 NL patterns: 'dead code', 'orphaned symbols', 'unreferenced functions', 'unreachable code', 'unused symbols', 'unused code', 'find orphan code', 'are there any orphans'. Oracle._execute_orphan_signal routes to GraphService.orphans. Fidelity=full for successful detection. Paper-writer: 104 orphans (72 methods, 19 functions, 11 inits, 2 classes). Key insight: method-level __init__ and dispatch-call methods appear as orphans because they lack explicit call-site edges. This is expected — they're entry points or dispatch targets. 9 new tests (7 classifier + 1 store + 1 integration), 120 total pass.

Hub detection: find architectural keystones with highest in-degree. GraphStore.find_hubs returns nodes sorted by incoming edge count across calls/imports/inherits. GraphService.hubs with node details and in_degree. CLI: 'trifecta graph hubs --top N'. HubPredicate in query classifier with 8 NL patterns: 'core abstractions', 'most depended upon symbols', 'blast radius', 'keystone modules', 'key components', 'what is most used', 'most used modules', 'which modules are most important'. Oracle._execute_hub_signal routes through graph signal path with fidelity=full. Paper-writer top keystones: ValidatorResult (in-degree=8), run_gate (8), ToolWrapper (7), Check (5), GateResult (5). Together with cycles and orphans, completes the architectural health triad: cycles=over-coupling, orphans=under-connection, hubs=blast radius. 9 new tests, 129 total pass.

Architectural overview query: composes cycles+orphans+hubs into unified health dashboard. OverviewPredicate in query classifier with 8 NL patterns: 'architectural overview', 'project health', 'codebase summary', 'how healthy is this codebase', 'health check', 'architecture review', 'give me an architecture overview', 'overall architecture'. Oracle._execute_overview_signal collects all three health metrics in one call: cycles across all 3 edge kinds, orphan count with kind breakdown, top 5 hubs with in-degree. CLI: 'trifecta graph overview'. Paper-writer overview: 0 cycles across calls/imports/inherits, 104 orphans (19 func, 72 method, 11 init, 2 class), top hubs: ValidatorResult(8), run_gate(8), ToolWrapper(7), StateManagerError(6), DomainStateError(5). Key design: OverviewPredicate is highest priority health query, checked before hub/orphan/cycle — it composes all three. 8 new tests (7 classifier + 1 integration), 137 total pass.

Path finding: shortest path between two symbols via BFS. GraphStore.find_path with BFS shortest-path algorithm across all edge kinds (calls/imports/inherits). GraphService.path resolves symbol names to candidates and finds shortest path across all combinations. CLI: 'trifecta graph path --from X --to Y'. PathPredicate in query classifier with 8 NL patterns: 'path from X to Y', 'how does X reach Y', 'how does X depend on Y', 'can X reach Y', 'does X depend on Y', 'path between X and Y', 'how is X connected to Y', 'dependency from X to Y'. Oracle._execute_path_signal with fidelity=full. Paper-writer paths: 'Orchestrator.execute to GateResult' → 4-hop chain (execute→_run_gate_verification→_run_wrapper_gate→GateResult); 'BibliographyNormalizer to ToolWrapper' → inheritance (2-hop); 'main to GateResult' → no direct path (class→method granularity gap). Key insight: path resolution at class-level misses method-level calls within the class — this is honest behavior, not a bug. 8 new tests (6 classifier + 1 store + 1 integration), 145 total pass.

Batch 1: Fix O-2 (6 failing segment canon tests) + implement O-4 (edge labels in paths). O-2 root cause: resolve_segment_state resolved relative repo_root='.' against process cwd instead of segment directory. Fix: check is_absolute() before resolve(). All 6 TestBuildContextPackUseCase tests now pass. O-4: GraphStore.find_path_with_edges returns hop-level dicts with from/to/edge_kind. GraphService.path includes new 'hops' field (backward compatible). Paper-writer paths now show edge types: Orchestrator.execute →[calls]→ _run_gate_verification →[calls]→ GateResult; BibliographyNormalizer →[inherits]→ ToolWrapper. 3 new tests for edge labels (mixed kinds, inheritance, no path). 128 total pass, 0 regressions.

name: O-1: Class→method path expansion

description: Baseline: O-1 class→method path gap verified. main→GateResult=False (should be True via Orchestrator→execute→…→GateResult). Orchestrator→GateResult=False. Method-level Orchestrator.execute→GateResult=True (3 hops). Direct call main→Orchestrator=True. Inheritance BibliographyNormalizer→ToolWrapper=True. 2 class-level paths fail due to 0 edges from class nodes to their methods.

O-1 RESOLVED: Class→method path gap completely fixed. Option C (implicit BFS expansion): when BFS reaches a class node, auto-expand its methods as zero-cost neighbors with 'owns' edge kind. No graph structure changes, no re-indexing, backward compatible (traverse_classes=True default, can disable for old behavior). _get_class_methods queries DB for method/init nodes by qualified_name prefix. Paper-writer: main→GateResult now returns True (was False): main→[calls]→Orchestrator→[owns]→Orchestrator._run_wrapper_gate→[calls]→GateResult. Orchestrator→GateResult now True (was False): Orchestrator→[owns]→Orchestrator._run_wrapper_gate→[calls]→GateResult. Oracle NL: 4/4 fidelity=full. Tests: 137 pass (+2 new), 0 regressions.

name: O-5: Reverse path / impact analysis

description: Baseline O-5: Impact/reverse-path queries partially work. 'blast radius' → hub query (in-degree), 'what depends on' → callers query. But 'impact of changing X' returns fidelity=fallback (no matching pattern). Key gap: no true reverse-path API showing ALL upstream nodes that can reach X across all edge kinds. Current callers only traverses call edges. No unified impact analysis combining calls+imports+inherits upstream.

O-5 RESOLVED: Reverse path / impact analysis fully implemented. GraphStore.find_upstream does reverse BFS across all edge kinds (calls+imports+inherits) with class→method reverse implicit traversal. GraphService.impact API resolves symbol to candidates, collects upstream nodes. CLI: 'trifecta graph impact X --depth N'. ImpactPredicate with 10 NL patterns (no overlap with existing relational patterns). Oracle._execute_impact_signal with fidelity=full. Paper-writer: 'impact of changing GateResult' -> 16 upstream nodes. 4 new unit tests, 141 total pass, 0 regressions.

name: O-7: Path length distribution in overview

description: O-7 RESOLVED: Path length distribution in overview. GraphStore.compute_path_length_stats does sampled BFS from up to 100 source nodes, returning avg_distance, max_distance, reachable_pairs, total_pairs, reachability_pct. GraphService.path_stats public API. CLI overview now includes path_stats. Oracle._execute_overview_signal includes path_stats in health dict. Paper-writer: avg=1.85 hops, max=5, reachability=1.6%. 1 new unit test, 142 total pass, 0 regressions.

name: O-10: CLI repo resolution

description: Baseline O-10: CLI graph subcommands require --segment flag. No auto-detection from cwd. Need to add --repo flag and/or auto-detect from current working directory.

O-10 RESOLVED: CLI repo auto-resolution. Added _resolve_repo in cli_graph.py: when --segment is '.' (default), walks up from cwd to find project root containing pyproject.toml, .git, or .trifecta/. Users can now run 'trifecta graph status' from anywhere inside a repo without --segment flag. All 20 CLI subcommands updated to use _resolve_repo. 0 regressions.

name: O-11: Persistent metrics

description: Baseline O-11: No persistent metrics API. Oracle returns fidelity + per-call latency but no accumulation. 8 query types measured: all return fidelity=full, latency 8-22ms. No way to track avg_latency, fidelity_rate, or p95 by signal_type over time.

O-11 RESOLVED: Persistent metrics via append-only JSONL. MetricsRecorder writes to .trifecta/metrics.jsonl per oracle call: ts, query_type, fidelity, latency_ms, timings. Aggregation computes per-type count, full/degraded/fallback counts, avg_latency, p95_latency. Oracle.execute auto-records metrics (best-effort, never blocks). CLI: 'trifecta graph metrics --last N'. Paper-writer: 8 calls recorded, 6 query types, all avg<30ms. 3 new unit tests, 119 total pass, 0 regressions.

name: O-9: LSP pre-warm

description: Baseline O-9: LSP signal never fires in CLI context. All 3 semantic queries return fidelity=degraded/fallback with no lsp_data. Root cause: pyright daemon is not running outside IDE. Pre-warm alone cannot fix this — the fundamental issue is that LSP requires a running language server process. This is an EXTERNAL DEPENDENCY gap, not a latency gap.

name: O-8: Auto-reindex

description: O-8 DEFERRED: Auto-reindex requires high-risk integration of GraphIndexer into Oracle query path. Current stale detection works graph is >7 days old → returns 'stale' signal → degraded fidelity. Adding auto-reindex on stale means: (1) GraphIndexer needs source_roots configuration at oracle call time, (2) reindex can take 5-10s blocking the query, (3) test surface is large. Defer to dedicated sprint.

name: Self-validation: does the oracle work on its own codebase?

description: Self-validation: 0/16 oracle queries work on trifecta_dope's own codebase. Root cause: graph indexed 2026-05-22, now 2026-06-01 (10 days old), past 7-day staleness threshold. All graph signals return 'stale' -> degraded fidelity. After re-indexing: 8/8 queries work. CRITICAL FINDING: O-8 (auto-reindex) is not optional — without it, the entire oracle is non-functional for any repo that hasn't been re-indexed in the last week. This means every CI run, every fresh checkout, every new repo starts in degraded state.
Phase 5: All 13 pre-existing ruff errors fixed (9 lint + 4 format). 532 tests, 0 regressions.

name: Doc accuracy: fix stale test counts and outdated blocker references
